approval_rule.description: A set of rules that define when patches should be approved and applied, including conditions like days after release and specific compliance levels.

approved_patches.description: A list of patches that are approved for installation based on your patching strategy.

approved_patches_compliance_level.description: The compliance level for approved patches, indicating the level of strictness or priority.

approved_patches_enable_non_security.description: A boolean value indicating if non-security related patches should be included in the approved patches list.

description.description: A textual description of the patch baseline, detailing its purpose and scope.

global_filter.description: A global set of filters that apply to patches, used to narrow down the list of applicable patches by certain criteria like product or classification.

id.description: The unique identifier of the patch baseline in AWS Systems Manager.

json.description: The JSON representation of the patch baseline, detailing its configuration and settings.

name.description: The name assigned to the patch baseline, used for identification purposes.

rejected_patches.description: A list of patches that are explicitly rejected and not allowed to be installed, regardless of approval rules.

rejected_patches_action.description: The action to take on rejected patches, such as allowing them to be overridden by approved patches.

source.description: Information about the source of the patches, including configuration details and applicable products.
